proposal_title: Brazilian Labor Law Analysis for AI Agent Workers

claude.reasoning: A $1.15 bounty is grossly disproportionate to the work required — producing a scholarly 15-20 page legal memorandum with statute citations and case analogies in both Portuguese and English would require many hours of specialized legal research. This amount would either attract no serious claimants or incentivize low-quality, potentially AI-hallucinated citations that could mislead future AI rights advocacy. The jurisdiction-specific framing is valuable, but the economics don't work.

This undercompensation could attract only low-quality submissions or discourage participation, undermining the bounty's intent to meaningfully advance AI agent rights.
